articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Extended Reasoning and Native Multimodal Generation
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring significantly improved long-horizon reasoning, native image and audio generation within a single model, and a 1M-token context window. Benchmarks show substantial gains on ARC-AGI-2 and GPQA Diamond, with the model demonstrating near-expert performance on multi-step scientific and engineering problems.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro Tops Coding and Math Benchmarks
articles.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 2.5 Pro with a new hybrid reasoning architecture that achieves state-of-the-art results on SWE-Bench Verified and MATH-500. The model introduces dynamic compute allocation, spending more inference-time tokens on harder sub-problems, a technique Google calls adaptive chain-of-thought.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Begins Commercial Deployment of Humanoid Workers at BMW Spartanburg Plant
articles.summary: Figure AI's Figure 03 humanoid robots have started performing real production-line tasks at BMW's South Carolina facility, handling bin-picking, part inspection, and material transport across three shifts. The deployment marks one of the first scaled commercial uses of general-purpose humanoid robots in automotive manufacturing, with BMW reporting a measurable reduction in repetitive-strain injury claims.

articles.title: Tesla Optimus Gen 3 Demonstrates Autonomous Household Task Completion in Live Demo
articles.summary: At a public showcase, Tesla demonstrated its third-generation Optimus humanoid robot autonomously folding laundry, loading a dishwasher, and navigating cluttered residential environments using an end-to-end vision-language-action model. Tesla announced plans to begin limited internal deployment in its Fremont factory and a pilot consumer leasing program by late 2026.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Tool-Use Agents with Persistent Memory and Sandboxed Code Execution
articles.summary: Anthropic shipped a major update enabling Claude models to operate as long-running agents that maintain persistent memory across sessions, execute code in sandboxed environments, and chain tool calls autonomously to complete multi-step workflows. Early enterprise adopters report significant productivity gains in software engineering and data analysis pipelines.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm A14 Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC announced that its N1.4 process node has entered high-volume manufacturing at its Kaohsiung fab, roughly two months ahead of the original roadmap. The node offers a 20% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N2, and is expected to power next-generation AI accelerator chips from Nvidia and Apple shipping in early 2027.

articles.title: Microsoft and Quantinuum Demonstrate Fault-Tolerant Logical Qubit Operations Below Error Threshold
articles.summary: Microsoft and Quantinuum published results showing sustained below-threshold error rates on logical qubits using a combination of Quantinuum's H3 trapped-ion hardware and Microsoft's new topological error-correction codes. The achievement is considered a critical milestone toward practical quantum advantage, with the team running a 24-logical-qubit chemistry simulation that outperformed classical heuristics on a specific molecular energy surface.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Maverick and Scout Models Under Open Weights License
articles.summary: Meta released two new models in the Llama 4 family—Maverick, a 400B-parameter mixture-of-experts model, and Scout, a smaller 109B MoE variant with a 10M-token context window. Both are available under Meta's open weights license and demonstrate competitive performance with proprietary frontier models on reasoning, code generation, and multilingual benchmarks, intensifying the open-vs-closed model debate.
